title: business-profile.json
description: S-32 / S-57 business-language requirements intake profile. schema_version 1.0 (universal 9) and 1.1 (S-57 full-surface intake) are BOTH valid: 1.1 is a strict additive superset — the universal-9 fields are unchanged and the classifier/probe/namespace-grounding fields are additive, so every v1.0 profile validates unchanged (§2.35 additivity invariant). The oneOf discriminates on schema_version: a 1.1 profile additionally requires workload_classification + probes + grounded_in_namespaces.

answers.description: The universal-9 (and optional S-38 data) answers — the source of truth in commands/business-intake.sh. Mirrored UNCHANGED into a v1.1 profile (universal-stays-universal).

grounded_in.description: Catalog source_ids the answered questions cite. In a v1.1 profile this is a STRICT SUPERSET of the v1.0 value (universal sources + answered-probe sources).

workload_classification.description: S-57 only. The classifier output: which workload_types fired, which full-surface namespaces they put in scope, and which of those have an activated probe group.

probes.description: S-57 only. Per-namespace probe answers: probes.<namespace>.<probe_key> = answer. Every key here belongs to an activated_probe_namespace.

grounded_in_namespaces.description: S-57 only. The full-surface namespaces grounded from a gathered probe answer (not a default). Each must appear in workload_classification.activated_probe_namespaces.

stack.description: §30.5/§30.6 — explicitly declared stack (via --stack-add). Each entry is a provenance object; idempotent by namespace.

full_surface.description: §30.7 — Stage-0 non-committing reveal of the whole rule surface. One entry per namespace, annotated activated + via.
